name: Logroño, Ebro Park, Stone Bridge – circular
name_original: Logroño, Parque del Ebro, Puente de Piedra – circular
region_intro: Jogging around Logroño offers diverse landscapes, from the meandering Ebro River and its riverside parks to the tranquil La Grajera Reservoir. The city's terrain features gentle urban paths, historic bridges, and accessible trails that cater to various fitness levels. Runners can explore green spaces, follow sections of the Camino de Santiago, or venture into the nearby hills for more challenging routes. This blend of urban and natural environments provides a rich setting for running activities. Best jogging routes around Logroño The most popular jogging route is Logroño, Ebro Park, Stone Bridge – circular, a 2.0 miles (3.3 km) trail that takes 21 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the Ebro River, offering views of the Stone Bridge and urban green spaces. Another top favourite among local runners is Swamp and La Grajera Park, Ebro Valley – circular, a moderate 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This route winds through the natural reserve around La Grajera Reservoir, providing a scenic experience in the Ebro Valley. Local runners also love the La Grajera Reservoir – La Grajera Reservoir loop from Estadio Municipal Las Gaunas, a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail leading through the natural reserve surrounding the reservoir, often completed in about 1 hour 7 minutes. Jogging around Logroño is defined by the Ebro River and its riverside parks, the La Grajera Reservoir, and sections of the Camino de Santiago. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy urban jogs to moderate trails. The routes in Logroño are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 3000 runners have used komoot to explore Logroño's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many jogging routes are available in Logroño?
A: Logroño offers a wide selection of over 180 jogging routes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These include a mix of urban paths, riverside trails, and routes through natural reserves.

Q: Are there easy running routes for beginners in Logroño?
A: Yes, Logroño features 24 easy running routes, ideal for beginners or those seeking a relaxed jog. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990059099/logrono-ebro-park-stone-bridge-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Logroño, Ebro Park, Stone Bridge – circular&lt;/a&gt; route, which is 2.0 miles (3.3 km) long and follows the Ebro River through urban green spaces.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails around Logroño?
A: While Logroño's immediate vicinity is known for accessible routes, there are 28 more difficult trails for those seeking a challenge. For more demanding terrain, you might consider venturing towards the Sierra de Cebollera Natural Park, a short distance from the city, which offers dense forests and varied trails.

Q: What are the options for running around La Grajera Reservoir?
A: The La Grajera Reservoir area is a popular natural oasis for runners. You can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990085336/swamp-and-la-grajera-park-ebro-valley-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Swamp and La Grajera Park, Ebro Valley – circular&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path, or the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14940153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Reservoir – La Grajera Reservoir loop from Estadio Municipal Las Gaunas&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.6 miles (10.6 km) and takes about 1 hour 7 minutes.

Q: Are there circular jogging routes in Logroño?
A: Absolutely! Many of Logroño's running routes are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same spot. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990059099/logrono-ebro-park-stone-bridge-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Logroño, Ebro Park, Stone Bridge – circular&lt;/a&gt; along the river, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990085336/swamp-and-la-grajera-park-ebro-valley-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Swamp and La Grajera Park, Ebro Valley – circular&lt;/a&gt; route around the reservoir.

Q: Can I jog on sections of the Camino de Santiago near Logroño?
A: Yes, Logroño is an important stop on the historic Camino de Santiago (French Way), providing unique trails for jogging. You can experience a section of this pilgrimage route with the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19884776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Santiago – La Grajera Reservoir loop from Estadio Municipal Las Gaunas&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.8 km (5.5 miles) trail.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while jogging in Logroño?
A: Logroño's jogging routes offer views of diverse natural features and landmarks. Along the Ebro River, you'll see the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/682737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Piedra (Stone Bridge)&lt;/a&gt; and Puente de Hierro (Iron Bridge). The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/682737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a significant natural oasis. For panoramic views, consider routes that lead to Tondeluna Viewpoint or Mecca Hill.

Q: Are there family-friendly jogging paths in Logroño?
A: Many of Logroño's urban green spaces and riverside paths are suitable for families. The flat, well-maintained paths of Parque del Ebro and Paseo del Espolón are excellent choices for a leisurely jog with children. The easy routes along the Ebro River are generally very accessible.

Q: Are dog-friendly running trails available in Logroño?
A: Yes, many of Logroño's natural areas and riverside paths are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy a run with your canine companion. The trails around Parque del Ebro and La Grajera Reservoir are popular choices for runners with dogs, but always ensure your dog is under control and check local regulations regarding leashes.

Q: What do other runners appreciate most about jogging in Logroño?
A: The komoot community highly rates Logroño's running routes, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, from the tranquil Ebro River paths to the scenic La Grajera Reservoir, and the blend of urban accessibility with natural beauty.

Q: What is the best season for running in Logroño?
A: Logroño offers pleasant running conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures, making them ideal for outdoor activities. Summer mornings and evenings are also popular, especially along the shaded riverside paths. Even in winter, many routes remain accessible, particularly the urban and riverside trails.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails in Logroño?
A: For longer runs, you can combine several routes or explore the extended trails around the La Grajera Reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40332527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Piedra de Logroño – Logroño Stone Bridge loop from Logroño&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 10.9 km (6.8 miles) distance, providing a good option for a longer workout.

Q: Can I access jogging routes in Logroño using public transport?
A: Many of Logroño's jogging routes, especially those within the city and along the Ebro River, are easily accessible via public transport. The urban green spaces and parks are well-connected, allowing you to reach starting points conveniently. For routes further out, like those around La Grajera, public transport options may vary, so checking local bus routes is advisable.
tips: The Revellín Walls are remains of the old fortifications of Logroño. The Cubo del Revellín (tower in the northwest corner) and the door on the west side have been preserved.
It is 198 meters long and has seven arches on cylindrical pillars. Its night lighting is spectacular.
La Redonda Co-Cathedral, next to the Plaza del Mercado, a shopping and leisure area, in the heart of the old town of Logroño. Very frequented and with a very good atmosphere.

waypoints.name: Cubo del Revellín and the Revellín Walls
Co-Cathedral of Santa María de la Redonda
